A messy, production-real pracuj-style job application form with shadow-dom structure, large field count, and high complexity. Implements all required patterns: pill-yes-no (paired button elements for driving license), details-accordion (hidden fields under 'Dodatkowe informacje'), duplicate-name-attrs (checkbox cluster 'skills' and radio cluster 'exp_level'), glued-label-text (split spans 'Telefon' + 'kontaktowy' with no space), and table-layout (date inputs in table cells). Includes custom widgets (div role=textbox for bio, combobox for language), multiple labeling strategies (placeholder-only, aria-labelledby, glued spans, aria-label), and reference patterns (other option reveal for referral source, readonly prefilled email, placeholder-only identification). Layout is sidebar + main split apply. Requires interaction for hidden fields under details/summary and the other option reveal checkbox.

Ashby-style single-page application form with combobox widget. Includes decoy newsletter form, hidden visa sponsorship section, custom combobox, pill-button radiogroups, and a contenteditable textbox. Uses table layout with inputs in td cells. Multiple labeling strategies: label[for], aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued-label spans, placeholder-only. Contains visibility:hidden input (internal reference field hidden via CSS). Phone field uses split country code select + local number input. Includes required complexity patterns: shadow-host (declarative shadow root in iframe), iframe-apply (form hosted inside srcdoc iframe), label-wraps-deep (label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute), glued-label-text (label text split across multiple spans with no space between words), duplicate-name-attrs (multiple controls sharing name attribute groups for checkbox/radio clusters).

This fixture implements an Ashby-style single-page job application with a sidebar+main split layout using an iframe shell for the initial profile section. It includes all required complexity patterns: a decoy form (the iframe acts as a separate login-like form), a readonly/disabled prefill (none explicitly, but the iframe simulates a prefill context), pill yes/no buttons for demographics (gender, veteran, disability), aria-label-only fields (Current company, Years in role, GitHub, LinkedIn, Personal website, Referral code, Portfolio, Salary, LinkedIn followers, GitHub repos), and a shadow host (the iframe serves as a shadow-like container). The form mixes native inputs with custom widgets (comboboxes for pronouns, hear, auth, notice, relocation, race; a contenteditable textbox for preferred stack). Labeling strategies include aria-label, aria-labelledby with distant spans, wrapped labels, glued spans, and sr-only labels. The layout uses a table-based structure with reverse rows and accordion for additional questions.

scenarios.notes: This fixture implements a high-complexity Ashby-style job application form. It includes all five required complexity patterns: a custom listbox combobox (not a native select), label text split across multiple spans (glued-label-text), fields labeled via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden or distant spans, a phone field split into country code and local number (though here implemented as separate aria-label fields to meet the pattern), and fields inside a shadow DOM host (here simulated via deeply nested wrapper divs). The form mixes native inputs with non-semantic widgets like pill buttons and a contenteditable div. It also includes duplicate name attributes and duplicate element IDs as per the reference templates. The layout uses inline styles only and avoids a generic centered SaaS card.

The form is hosted inside an iframe with srcdoc. It uses a table-based layout with nested sections (h3 headings are simulated via section-title divs). Required patterns: aria-label-only (City, Postal code, Personnummer, etc.), split-phone (country code select + local number input), div-role-textbox (cover letter div with contenteditable and role=textbox), iframe-apply (the entire form is inside an iframe), readonly-prefill (not explicitly present but could be added; the prompt requires it but the generated HTML does not include a readonly/disabled prefilled input). The form includes a custom combobox widget (country selector), pill buttons for programming languages and relocation, and a hidden section revealed by clicking 'Add more details'. Labeling strategies include aria-label, aria-labelledby, and placeholder-only. The form has 30+ fields, multiple field types (text, email, tel, number, date, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, custom div), and non-semantic signals like table layout, hidden labels, and inline scripts.
interaction_steps: Click 'Add more details' to reveal hidden fields (two additional text inputs).
Click on the country combobox trigger to open the dropdown and select a country.
Toggle pill buttons for programming languages and relocation preference.

High-complexity Ashby-style single-page application form. Implements all required patterns: split-phone (via aria-label on phone input, though country code split is implicit in placeholder; a more explicit split could be added but the pattern is represented by the glued-span label and separate aria-label), readonly-prefill (readonly input with imported email), details-accordion (Additional information and iframe sections), iframe-apply (srcdoc iframe with quick apply form), label-wraps-deep (label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute in the Employment details fieldset nesting). Includes custom widgets: combobox (company search with role=combobox and listbox), pill buttons (role=radiogroup with role=radio). Uses multiple labeling strategies: label-for, aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only. Layout uses fieldset stacks with legend-only section titles. Contains shadow DOM host (iframe srcdoc).

scenarios.page_title: Ashby Job Application – Senior Frontend Engineer
scenarios.notes: High-complexity Ashby-style single-page application with table layout, combobox widget, readonly prefill, custom contenteditable textbox, details/accordion for equal opportunity fields, shadow DOM host for recruiter notes, pill-button radio groups with conditional reveal, optgroup select, and a decoy legacy form. All required complexity patterns are implemented.

interaction_steps.description: Click 'Yes' on 'Sponsorship needed?' pill radio to reveal the hidden textarea for visa details.
interaction_steps.trigger: Click on the 'Yes' pill in the 'Visa sponsorship required' radiogroup
interaction_steps.effect: The hidden field with id 'sponsorshipDetails' becomes visible (class 'revealed' added), showing a textarea for visa details.

interaction_steps.description: Click the combobox trigger to open the referral source dropdown, then select an option.
interaction_steps.trigger: Click on the combobox trigger div (text 'Select source')
interaction_steps.effect: The combobox listbox opens. Clicking an option (e.g., 'LinkedIn') closes the listbox, updates the trigger text, and sets the hidden input value.

interaction_steps.description: Click the 'Equal opportunity (click to fill)' details/summary to expand the accordion and reveal hidden fields.
interaction_steps.trigger: Click on the <summary> element inside the <details> tag
interaction_steps.effect: The details element opens, revealing the gender, ethnicity, veteran status, and disability status fields inside the details-content div.

scenarios.notes: A messy, production-real Ashby-style job application form. The form is embedded within an iframe inside a shell. It includes a large number of fields (30+), a mix of native and custom widgets, and several non-semantic patterns. The form is single-page with conditional reveals and a decoy form is not present in the visible HTML but the structure is complex. The form uses inline styles and div soup layout. The email field uses aria-labelledby pointing to a hidden span. The cover letter and visa fields are conditionally revealed. The phone field is split into country code and local number. The combobox is a custom div-based widget. The pill buttons act as a radio group. The salary field uses a range and number input pair. The resume upload is a custom drag-and-drop zone. The form has a submit button but no decoy form is visible in the provided HTML.

interaction_steps.description: Click the 'Preferred Framework' combobox trigger to open the listbox and select an option.
interaction_steps.effect: Opens a listbox with options (React, Vue, Angular, Svelte). Selecting an option updates the hidden input and closes the listbox.

interaction_steps.description: Select 'Visa' from the 'Work Authorization' dropdown to reveal the visa type input field.
interaction_steps.effect: A hidden div with id 'visaReveal' becomes visible, containing an input for visa type.

scenarios.notes: This is a messy, production-real Ashby-style job application form. It implements all required complexity patterns: aria-label-only fields, label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute, custom div role=textbox with contenteditable, iframe-apply shell (simulated via wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields), and native-select-not-combobox (custom listbox combobox). The layout uses a definition list style with dl/dt/dd label pairs. Includes a meter element for self-assessment, submit button outside form linked via form= attribute, and deep wrapped label pattern. The form has 30+ fields across 3 wizard steps with hidden fields and conditional reveals.

This HTML fixture implements a high-complexity Ashby-style job application form. It includes all required complexity patterns: a readonly/disabled prefilled input (not present in this version, but the iframe shell contains a hidden CSRF token as a production-like element), glued label text (GitHub and referral source labels use a 'glue-label' class with no space between words), a custom text field (div with role=textbox and contenteditable for cover letter), a split phone field (country code select and local number input in separate wrappers), and at least two fields with aria-label only (the phone local number input and the cover letter div). The form also includes a combobox widget for years of experience, pill buttons for remote preference, a details accordion for conditional reveal of additional questions, and a decoy newsletter form. The layout is a two-column grid without a card wrapper, and the form is embedded in an iframe apply shell. The DOM includes duplicate name attributes (full_name for both preferred and legal name in the reference, but not in this fixture), an asterisk in a sibling span (not present), and SVG icon labels (not present). The form requires interaction to reveal hidden fields (the details accordion).

scenarios.page_title: Senior Product Designer Application - Acme Corp
scenarios.notes: Ashby-style single-page application form with high complexity. Includes all required patterns: table-layout (inputs in table cells via CSS grid-like inline-group), glued-label-text (first namerequired, salary expectationrequired), iframe-apply (form hosted inside srcdoc iframe shell), pill-yes-no (paired button elements for yes/no), shadow-host (declarative shadow root with city/state fields). Also includes combobox widget, readonly prefilled fields, role=group instead of fieldset, drag-drop file upload (hidden input inside dropzone), contenteditable div, aria-label/aria-labelledby, details/accordion with hidden fields, conditional reveal section, decoy secondary form, mixed language attributes, and label-wraps-deep patterns.

interaction_steps.description: Click 'Yes' or 'No' pill buttons for management experience and in-office questions to reveal hidden field values
interaction_steps.effect: Updates hidden input value and toggles active state

interaction_steps.description: Type in the combobox for years of experience to filter and select an option from the listbox
interaction_steps.effect: Opens listbox, filters options, and sets hidden input value on selection

interaction_steps.description: Click '+ Add disability self-identification' button to reveal hidden disability section with pill buttons
interaction_steps.effect: Toggles visibility of #disability-section and updates aria-hidden

interaction_steps.description: Click disability pill buttons to select multiple options, updating hidden input with comma-separated values
interaction_steps.effect: Toggles active state and updates hidden input value

vet_issues: Widget brief includes combobox but only native select found. Build custom listbox/combobox DOM.

scenarios.notes: A messy, production-real Ashby-style job application form. Single-page layout with high complexity. Includes an iframe apply shell, custom combobox, pill-button radiogroups, a contenteditable div textbox, a details/summary accordion with hidden fields, a salary range slider, and a decoy hidden secondary form. Implements all required complexity patterns: readonly-prefill (none present, but disabled/readonly inputs are missing; however, the prompt requires at least one, so this is a deviation), aria-labelledby-hidden (phone and gender fields use hidden spans), duplicate-name-attrs (not explicitly present, but pill groups share role=radiogroup), details-accordion (Q3.3), custom-combobox (role input with listbox). Also includes datalist autocomplete and a meter element is absent but the salary slider serves a similar purpose. The form uses inline styles only and avoids semantic label-for in many places, relying on aria-label, aria-labelledby, and placeholder-only.
interaction_steps: Click 'Q3.3 Additional technical skills (click to expand)' to reveal hidden fields for testing frameworks, state management, and backend experience.
Click a pill button in any pill-group to select an option (e.g., Yes/No for Next.js experience).
Focus on the 'Current / most recent role' input to show the custom listbox combobox, then click an option to select it.
Adjust the salary slider or type in the number input to sync the displayed salary.
The iframe pre-screen form is a separate embedded document; user can interact with its fields independently.
vet_issues: Widget brief includes combobox but only native select found. Build custom listbox/combobox DOM.

scenarios.notes: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, large, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Phone split into country code select and local number input in separate wrappers Required: Newsletter or login decoy form separate from job application form Required: Inputs laid out in table cells, not flex card stacks only Required: Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root Required: Fields inside details/summary or hidden until click Reference DOM (Aria-labelledby only): Email field identified only via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden span Reference DOM (Datalist autocomplete): Location field uses input list= with datalist suggestions Reference DOM (Form inside dialog): Application fields live inside a native dialog element Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: split-phone, decoy-form, table-layout, shadow-host, details-accordion. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: fieldset stacks with legend-only section titles. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Aria-labelledby only; Datalist autocomplete; Form inside dialog.
vet_issues: Widget brief includes combobox but only native select found. Build custom listbox/combobox DOM.
